main module

class main.BetRequest(*, bet: int, fund_source: str = 'real')[source]

Bases: BaseModel

Model for placing a bet (e.g., in Blackjack).

bet

Wager amount.

Type:

int

fund_source

Source of funds (“real” or “bonus”). Defaults to “real”.

Type:

str

bet: int
fund_source: str
model_config = {}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

class main.PaymentRequest(*, amount: int, method: str, promo_code: str = '')[source]

Bases: BaseModel

Model for deposit requests.

amount

Amount to deposit.

Type:

int

method

Payment method (e.g., “Card”, “BLIK”).

Type:

str

promo_code

Optional promo code.

Type:

str

amount: int
method: str
model_config = {}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

promo_code: str
class main.RouletteBetRequest(*, bet_amount: int, fund_source: str = 'real', bet_type: str, bet_number: int = -1)[source]

Bases: BaseModel

Model for placing a bet in Roulette.

bet_amount

Wager amount.

Type:

int

fund_source

Source of funds (“real” or “bonus”).

Type:

str

bet_type

Type of bet (“red”, “black”, “even”, “odd”, “low”, “high”, “number”).

Type:

str

bet_number

Chosen number (if bet_type is “number”). Defaults to -1.

Type:

int

bet_amount: int
bet_number: int
bet_type: str
fund_source: str
model_config = {}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

class main.Transaction(**kwargs)[source]

Bases: Base

Database model representing a single financial transaction. Used for logging deposits, withdrawals, and challenge rewards.

amount
date
id
method
status
type
user
user_id
class main.User(**kwargs)[source]

Bases: Base

Database model representing a user (player). Stores authentication data, balances (real and bonus), and challenge progression.

balance
bonus_balance
chal_deposit_claimed
chal_hands_claimed
chal_wager_claimed

hands_played
id
last_name
password
total_wagered
transactions

Bases: BaseModel

Model for user login.

email

Email address.

Type:

str

password

Password.

Type:

str

email: str
model_config = {}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

password: str

Bases: BaseModel

Model for registering a new user.

first_name

First name.

str

Last name.

str

str

Password.

str

last_name: str

class main.WithdrawRequest(*, amount: int)[source]

Bases: BaseModel

Model for withdrawal requests.

amount

Amount to withdraw.

Type:

int

amount: int
model_config = {}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

main.calculate_points(hand)[source]

Calculates the total points for a given Blackjack hand. An Ace counts as 11, unless the total exceeds 21, in which case it counts as 1.

Parameters:

hand (list) – A list of card strings belonging to the player or dealer.

Returns:

The optimized point total for the provided hand.

Return type:

int

main.claim_challenge(email: str, challenge_id: str)[source]

Claims a reward for completing a specific challenge tier. The player’s challenge level increments after a successful claim, opening up the next tier.

Parameters:

  • email (str) – The player’s email address.

  • challenge_id (str) – The identifier for the challenge (“wager”, “hands”, “deposit”).

Raises:

HTTPException – If conditions aren’t met or the ID is unrecognized.

Returns:

A confirmation message of the successful claim.

Return type:

dict

main.create_deck()[source]

Creates and shuffles a standard 52-card deck (e.g., for Blackjack).

Returns:

A shuffled list of strings representing cards (e.g., [‘2♠’, ‘K♥’, ‘A♣’, …]).

Return type:

list

main.double(email: str)[source]

The ‘Double Down’ action - double the wager, draw exactly ONE card, and automatically stand.

Parameters:

email (str) – The player’s email address.

Raises:

HTTPException – If game not found, wrong timing (only allowed on initial 2 cards), or lacking funds.

Returns:

The updated game state (or final results if this was the last hand).

Return type:

dict

main.end_game(email)[source]

Ends the Blackjack game. The dealer draws cards, results are verified for each player hand, and winnings are paid out.

Parameters:

email (str) – The player’s email address.

Returns:

Complete game results, final dealer cards, and updated account balances.

Return type:

dict

main.get_game_state(session, email)[source]

Helper function to build an object representing the current Blackjack game state.

Parameters:

  • session (dict) – The active game session for the user.

  • email (str) – The player’s email address.

Returns:

A parsed object detailing cards on the table, point totals, and wager amounts.

Return type:

dict

main.get_history(email: str)[source]

Fetches the transaction history for a user, sorted from newest to oldest.

Parameters:

email (str) – The player’s email address.

Returns:

A list of dictionaries detailing the transactions for the Cashier view.

Return type:

dict

main.get_state(email: str)[source]

Retrieves the full account state of a player, including any active game session.

Parameters:

email (str) – The player’s email address.

Raises:

HTTPException – If the player does not exist.

Returns:

A summary of balances, challenge stats, and the current active game state (if any).

Return type:

dict

main.hit(email: str)[source]

The ‘Hit’ action in Blackjack - the player draws another card from the deck.

Parameters:

email (str) – The player’s email address.

Raises:

HTTPException – If no active game session is found.

Returns:

The updated game state, or triggers end_game if the player busts on their final hand.

Return type:

dict

main.login(data: UserAuth)[source]

Endpoint for user login.

Parameters:

data (UserAuth) – Login credentials (email, password).

Returns:

Account details upon successful login or an error message.

Return type:

dict

async main.main_page()[source]

Endpoint serving the main HTML page.

Returns:

Response containing the index.html file, bypassing browser cache.

Return type:

FileResponse

main.make_deposit(email: str, data: PaymentRequest)[source]

Endpoint handling a deposit of funds into the player’s real money balance. Supports promotional codes (e.g., entering START yields a $50 bonus).

Parameters:

  • email (str) – The player’s email address.

  • data (PaymentRequest) – Payment details.

Raises:

HTTPException – On invalid amount or if the player isn’t found.

Returns:

Success message alongside updated balances.

Return type:

dict

main.make_withdraw(email: str, data: WithdrawRequest)[source]

Submits a withdrawal request to cash out the player’s real funds.

Parameters:

  • email (str) – The player’s email address.

  • data (WithdrawRequest) – Model defining the amount to withdraw.

Raises:

HTTPException – On invalid amount or insufficient funds.

Returns:

Success message and the updated real money balance.

Return type:

dict

main.register(data: UserRegister)[source]

Endpoint for registering a new player.

Parameters:

data (UserRegister) – Form data received for registration.

Returns:

A success message or an error if the email is already taken.

Return type:

dict

main.spin_roulette(email: str, data: RouletteBetRequest)[source]

Spins the European roulette wheel, calculating payouts based on the bet type, and modifying the player’s balances accordingly. All results are simulated pseudo-randomly on the backend; the frontend merely visualizes it!

Parameters:

  • email (str) – The player’s email address.

  • data (RouletteBetRequest) – Object representing the bet type and wager amount.

Raises:

HTTPException – On insufficient funds.

Returns:

The final spin result, color, payout, and updated balances.

Return type:

dict

main.split(email: str)[source]

The ‘Split’ action - divide a pair into two separate hands with identical base wagers, provided the first two cards share the same point value (e.g., two 8s, or a 10 and a King).

Parameters:

email (str) – The player’s email address.

Raises:

HTTPException – If no active game, wrong timing, mismatched card values, or insufficient funds.

Returns:

The game state reflecting the newly formed split hands.

Return type:

dict

main.stand(email: str)[source]

The ‘Stand’ action in Blackjack - the player decides to take no more cards for the current hand.

Parameters:

email (str) – The player’s email address.

Raises:

HTTPException – If no active game session is found.

Returns:

Game state moving to the next hand, or triggers end_game.

Return type:

dict

main.start_game(email: str, data: BetRequest)[source]

Starts a new game of Blackjack and places the player’s wager.

Parameters:

  • email (str) – The player’s email address.

  • data (BetRequest) – Betting data.

Raises:

HTTPException – If the wager amount is invalid or funds are insufficient.

Returns:

The state of the game right after dealing the first cards.

Return type:

dict
